First, I need to figure out how to use the bash tool here. The bash tool's command should be able to list all hidden files in that directory. Hidden files are usually those that start with a dot. So the command would probably be something like 'find /path/to/directory -name ".*" -type f -exec rm {} +'.

But the user's exact request is to delete hidden files. So the correct command should be 'find /testdata/workspace -name '.*' -type f -exec rm {} +'.
